Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Let me analyze the log: **Raw Events Chronologically:** 1. 08:59:50 - FOCUS Word: Quarterly_Report.docx 2. 09:00:00 - FOCUS Word: Document1.docx ... 26. 09:08:15 - CLOSE Word: Quarterly_Report.docx

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: **Case Identification Logic:** There are several approaches: - By document/window (most logical for knowledge work) - By application session - By time window (e.g., everything within a work session) I think the most analyst-friendly approach for process mining is to group by the **document/file/email** being worked on.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Document1.docx: Events 2-5, 19-22. This is a clear case: Open/focus, type, save, switch away, switch back, type, save, close. ... This is actually a very realistic process: Draft Document1 involving research/reference gathering. So cases by document/window title seem best

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: should Document1.docx be split because the user switched away? In process mining, a case can have gaps. ... SWITCH events as context transitions. Ill assign them to the case being switched TO as Access Document

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Activity Naming ... having too many specific activities makes the model spaghetti. Standard approach is to generalize. ... Let me decide on the final activity list: ... Now for the Review Content activity in EMAIL001 vs PDF001. Should I differentiate?

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: **Universal/Standardized:** - TYPING  Edit Content (for Word, Excel) or Compose Reply (for Email) - SAVE  Save Document ... This is the most standard for process mining.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: All 26 events accounted for. Great! ... One final check: Event 6 mapping. ... Now, about Case IDs. ... Alright, Im ready to write the response.
